name: Gemini 1.0 Pro
tagline: Google's original mid-tier Gemini model — the engine behind the 2023 Bard upgrade.
seoTitle: Gemini 1.0 Pro — Google LLM Specs & Benchmarks
metaDescription: Gemini 1.0 Pro (Google), released December 2023 — specs, MMLU/GSM8K/HumanEval benchmarks, pricing and API details for Google's original mid-tier Gemini model,…
modalities: Vision (image input, via Gemini 1.0 Pro Vision)
overview: Gemini 1.0 Pro was Google's mid-tier model in the first Gemini family, announced on December 6, 2023 and made available to developers on December 13, 2023 through Google AI Studio and the Vertex AI Gemini API. Positioned between the lightweight Gemini Nano and the flagship Gemini Ultra, Gemini 1.0 Pro was pitched as the best model for scaling across a wide range of everyday text, reasoning, and coding tasks. A fine-tuned version of Gemini Pro also powered the largest upgrade to Google's Bard chatbot since its launch.

Unlike the text-only assistants of its era, the Gemini line was designed to be natively multimodal. The base gemini-1.0-pro endpoint handled text in and text out, while a companion gemini-1.0-pro-vision variant accepted image input alongside text. The model shipped with roughly a 32K-token context window and an 8,192-token maximum output, modest by today's standards but competitive at launch. Google described Gemini Pro as outperforming GPT-3.5-class models on most academic benchmarks.

Gemini 1.0 Pro has since been retired. Google moved developers onto the far more capable Gemini 1.5 (with its million-token context) and later the Gemini 2.x and 3.x generations, and the 1.0 Pro endpoints were marked unsupported/discontinued across Google AI Studio and Vertex AI in 2025. The model remains historically important as the version that first put the Gemini brand in front of developers and Bard users.
strengths: Natively multimodal design — the Gemini 1.0 Pro Vision variant accepted images alongside text
Strong grade-school math reasoning for its tier (86.5% on GSM8K in the Gemini technical report)
Broad general-knowledge and reasoning coverage (71.8% MMLU), beating GPT-3.5-class models at launch
Available day one through both Google AI Studio and enterprise-grade Vertex AI
Low, predictable token pricing that made it cheap to scale across high-volume text tasks
useCases: Multi-turn text and code chat assistants
Code generation and explanation in popular programming languages
Summarization, classification, and rewriting over natural-language text
Image understanding and visual Q&A (via the Gemini 1.0 Pro Vision variant)
Powering Bard-style conversational reasoning and planning features

links.label: Vertex AI model versions and lifecycle

status: Deprecated — discontinued on Google AI Studio and Vertex AI in 2025; superseded by Gemini 1.5 and later.

pricing.note: Paid Gemini API token pricing for the gemini-1.0-pro text model; model now retired.

faq.a: Google announced Gemini on December 6, 2023, and made Gemini 1.0 Pro available to developers on December 13, 2023 through Google AI Studio and the Vertex AI Gemini API. A fine-tuned version also powered a major upgrade to the Bard chatbot.

No. Gemini 1.0 Pro has been deprecated and discontinued. Google retired the 1.0 Pro endpoints on Google AI Studio and Vertex AI in 2025, directing developers to newer models such as Gemini 1.5, 2.x and 3.x.

faq.q: What context window does Gemini 1.0 Pro have?
faq.a: Gemini 1.0 Pro supported roughly a 32K-token context window (about 32,760 input tokens) with a maximum output of 8,192 tokens — modest compared with the million-token context introduced later in Gemini 1.5.

faq.q: How did Gemini 1.0 Pro perform on benchmarks?
faq.a: In Google's Gemini technical report the Pro tier scored 71.8% on MMLU, 86.5% on GSM8K, 67.7% on HumanEval and 75.0% on BIG-Bench-Hard — positioning it above GPT-3.5-class models while the larger Gemini Ultra led the family.
